record.source_version: Studio inspiration-block catalog retrieved via MCP
modifications: Replaced the source's Avatar/AvatarFallback icon chip with a plain styled span carrying the same token classes; the platform does not own an Avatar primitive and one icon chip does not justify importing a new primitive system

Separated the source's single trend prop into trend (arrow direction, a fact) and tone (positive/negative/neutral interpretation) so a falling defect count can trend down with a positive tone; tone maps to the governed status-role tokens (text-status-success/critical), never raw palette

Added the canonical states the source lacks: loading (aria-hidden owned Skeleton), errorText, and stale (aria-hidden visible 'Stale' text); all three are announced through one persistent aria-live region rather than a role=status element mounted only once its content already exists, which is not reliably announced

Added sr-only trend-direction text ('increase'/'decrease'/'no change') and a visible tone word ('Improving'/'Worsening'/'Steady') so delta direction AND meaning are both stated in text, not color alone, per DASHBOARD_AND_DATA_VISUALIZATION.md's compact-KPI anatomy ('green or red alone is insufficient')

Renamed badgeContent to periodText (the governed time window/comparison basis) and added optional freshnessText; the anatomy's required 'evidence link' and 'partial' state are not implemented, disclosed as an open gap in the catalog entry rather than invented without a real consuming surface

Changed Card and Badge imports from generic shadcn '@/components/ui/*' aliases to the platform's owned '@meridian/ui-web/components/*'; added the owned Skeleton import for the new loading state (the source had no loading state, so this is a new import, not a changed one)
Extracted the value/loading/error branch into a MetricCardValue helper to satisfy the platform's noNestedTernary lint rule
Dropped the source's page.tsx route wrapper and hardcoded demo data; this is a reusable primitive, not a standalone route

Changed the 'Stale' indicator's className from text-status-warning to text-status-warning-foreground (2026-07-23, issue #209) -- status-warning itself reaches only 4.92:1 against white with no headroom once tinted/blended; the new -foreground token (7.09:1) is the AA-safe value for colored warning text specifically, per docs/blueprint/09-UX/DESIGN_TOKEN_VALUES_AND_BREAKPOINTS.md
